title: Cursor teams standardize on MCP as the default agent perimeter
excerpt: More Cursor users are wiring GitHub, docs, and database MCP servers into project config — replacing one-off REST scripts and paste-into-chat debugging loops.
contentHtml:
The Cursor workflow is splitting cleanly: REST inside your services, MCP at the IDE edge. Teams share .cursor/mcp.json for approved connectors — GitHub, Context7, read-only Postgres — and stop rebuilding debug glue every sprint.

Common pattern we hear:

  1. Agent reads the repo (baseline)
  2. MCP tools reach staging data, issues, and live docs
  3. Incidents that took an afternoon of cURL now close in twenty minutes

Why it matters: model quality is table stakes. Tool reach is the new bottleneck — and MCP is how Cursor users buy it back.

Read the story: The afternoon I stopped writing glue code. Setup guide: First MCP servers in Cursor.
